frameworks.name: hippocampal cognitive map
frameworks.one_line: CA1 place cells implement a map of space supporting flexible navigation

frameworks.name: place cell coding
frameworks.one_line: Location-specific firing of dorsal CA1 neurons indexes spatial position

frameworks.name: environmental connectivity representation
frameworks.one_line: Possibility that place cells encode available routes between subspaces

core_question: Do dorsal CA1 place cells encode the connectivity structure between adjoining subspaces in addition to location?
key_assumptions: Place cells provide the neural substrate of a cognitive spatial map
Doors that lock/unlock manipulate connectivity without changing geometry
Behavior in the apparatus reflects knowledge of connectivity
manipulations: Four geometrically identical boxes arranged in a square
Saloon doors lockable in one or both directions to alter connectivity
Compared place-cell activity at doorways vs other locations
key_findings: Rats behaviorally demonstrated knowledge of environmental connectivity
Place cells did not respond to connectivity changes
Doorways were not represented differently from other locations
A distinct map appeared for each box with minimal repetitive fields despite identical geometry
interpretations: CA1 place cells code location in a global reference frame rather than encoding connectivity
The hippocampal spatial map does not explicitly include environmental connectivity
